title: Merge PDF Files for Students — Free, No Watermark
description: Combine assignment PDFs, lecture notes, and research papers into one document. Runs in your browser — your academic files stay private.
steps: Open JustUse.me Merge PDF — no signup needed
Drag your assignment PDFs, notes, or papers into the drop zone
Reorder pages to match your submission requirements
Click Merge and download your combined PDF

faq.q: Can I merge my assignment PDFs without creating an account?
faq.a: Yes. JustUse.me requires no account for free usage. Just drop your files and merge.

faq.q: Will my professor's documents be uploaded to a server?
faq.a: No. All processing happens in your browser. Your files never leave your device.

faq.q: Is there a page limit for merging?
faq.a: You can merge up to 20 PDFs, each up to 30MB. That covers most academic documents.

title: Merge Legal PDF Documents — Secure, Browser-Based
description: Combine contracts, exhibits, and case documents into a single PDF ready for filing or client delivery. Processing happens entirely in your browser — no files are sent to any server.
steps: Open JustUse.me Merge PDF — no account required
Upload your contracts, exhibits, or court documents
Drag to reorder documents in the correct legal sequence
Click Merge and download the consolidated PDF

faq.q: Are my client documents secure when I use this tool?
faq.a: Yes. All processing is done locally in your browser. No files are transmitted to or stored on any server.

faq.q: Can I control the order of documents before merging?
faq.a: Yes. After uploading, drag and drop to arrange documents in any order before merging.

faq.q: Does the merged PDF preserve bookmarks and hyperlinks?
faq.a: The merged PDF preserves the text and page structure from your source documents. Complex internal bookmarks may not carry over depending on the original files.

title: Merge PDF for Email Attachment — Combine Files Before Sending
description: Turn multiple PDF attachments into one clean file before emailing clients or colleagues. Smaller, neater, and less likely to hit email attachment limits.
steps: Open JustUse.me Merge PDF in your browser
Upload the PDFs you want to send as one attachment
Reorder documents if needed
Download the merged PDF and attach it to your email

faq.q: Will merging multiple PDFs reduce the file size?
faq.a: Merging combines files but does not compress them. If you need a smaller file, use the Compress PDF tool after merging.

faq.q: Can I merge PDFs on my phone before emailing?
faq.a: Yes. JustUse.me works in mobile browsers on iPhone and Android — no app download needed.

faq.q: Is there a file size limit for email-ready PDFs?
faq.a: You can merge PDFs up to 30MB each. Most email providers accept attachments up to 25MB, so compress after merging if needed.

title: Merge PDF Free — No Watermark, No Sign-Up
description: Combine PDF files for free without any watermark added to your document. No account, no trial, no catch — just a clean merged PDF every time.
steps: Go to JustUse.me Merge PDF
Drop your PDF files into the upload area
Arrange pages in the order you need
Click Merge — download a clean PDF with no watermarks

faq.q: Why do other free tools add watermarks?
faq.a: Most tools add watermarks to push you toward paid plans. JustUse.me is free and adds no watermarks.

faq.q: Do I need to create an account to get a watermark-free PDF?
faq.a: No account needed. You get a clean merged PDF every time, completely free.

faq.q: How many PDFs can I merge for free?
faq.a: You can merge up to 20 PDFs per session, each up to 30MB, at no cost.

title: Merge PDF on iPhone — No App Required
description: Combine PDF files directly from your iPhone using Safari or Chrome — no app download needed. Works with files from Files, Google Drive, or your camera roll.
steps: Open Safari or Chrome on your iPhone and go to JustUse.me
Tap the upload area and select your PDFs from Files or iCloud
Reorder documents by dragging if needed
Tap Merge and save the combined PDF to your Files app

faq.q: Can I merge PDFs on iPhone without downloading an app?
faq.a: Yes. JustUse.me is a web-based tool that works in your iPhone's browser with no app required.

faq.q: How do I select PDF files from iCloud on my iPhone?
faq.a: Tap the upload area, then choose Files. Navigate to iCloud Drive and select your PDFs.

faq.q: Where does the merged PDF save on my iPhone?
faq.a: After downloading, the PDF goes to your Downloads folder in the Files app, or you can share it directly to another app.

title: Compress PDF Under 1MB — Fast, Free, Online
description: Reduce your PDF file size to under 1MB for portals, forms, and systems with strict upload limits. No software installation required — works in any browser.
steps: Open JustUse.me Compress PDF
Upload your PDF file
Choose a compression level — Higher Compression for the smallest file size
Download your compressed PDF and check the file size

faq.q: Can I guarantee my PDF will be under 1MB after compression?
faq.a: Compression results depend on the content of your PDF. Image-heavy PDFs compress significantly; text-only PDFs are already small. Try High Compression mode first.

faq.q: Will compressing under 1MB make my PDF unreadable?
faq.a: Text remains sharp. Images may show slight quality reduction at high compression, but documents remain fully legible.

faq.q: What if the PDF is still over 1MB after compression?
faq.a: Try splitting the PDF into smaller sections using a PDF split tool, then compress each part separately.

title: Compress PDF for Email — Reduce File Size Before Sending
description: Shrink large PDFs so they fit within email attachment limits without bouncing back. Compresses in your browser in seconds — no software needed.
steps: Open JustUse.me Compress PDF in your browser
Upload the PDF you want to email
Select a compression level based on how much you need to reduce
Download the smaller PDF and attach it to your email

faq.q: What is the typical email attachment size limit?
faq.a: Gmail and Outlook both cap attachments at 25MB. Aim for under 20MB to be safe with most providers.

faq.q: Will the compressed PDF look different to the recipient?
faq.a: Text quality is preserved. If your PDF contains photos, very high compression may slightly reduce image sharpness.

faq.q: Can I compress a PDF on my phone before emailing it?
faq.a: Yes. JustUse.me works in mobile browsers — open it on your iPhone or Android, upload, compress, and share directly.

title: Compress PDF Without Losing Quality — Smart Optimization
description: Reduce PDF file size while keeping text sharp and images clear. Uses lossless optimization to strip hidden data rather than degrading your content.
steps: Open JustUse.me Compress PDF
Upload your PDF
Select Low Compression to prioritize quality over file size
Download and compare — text and images should look identical

faq.q: What does low compression do differently?
faq.a: Low compression removes redundant metadata, embedded fonts, and unused resources without altering images. Quality stays the same.

faq.q: Is there any compression that is truly lossless for PDFs?
faq.a: Yes. Removing metadata and optimizing internal structure does not affect visual quality. Only image downsampling causes visible quality loss.

faq.q: My PDF has charts and diagrams — will they stay sharp?
faq.a: Vector graphics like charts stay pixel-perfect at any compression level. Raster images may soften only under high compression settings.

title: Compress PDF Free — No File Limit, No Watermark
description: Compress as many PDFs as you need, completely free, with no daily caps or watermarks added. No account needed — just upload and download.
steps: Go to JustUse.me Compress PDF
Upload your PDF — no account or sign-up required
Pick your compression level
Download your smaller PDF — repeat as many times as needed

faq.q: Is there a daily limit on how many PDFs I can compress?
faq.a: No. JustUse.me does not cap the number of files you can process per day.

faq.q: Will a watermark appear on my compressed PDF?
faq.a: No watermarks are added. You get a clean, unmodified compressed PDF.

faq.q: Do I need to pay or subscribe to use this?
faq.a: No payment or subscription is required. The tool is free for everyone.

title: Compress Scanned PDF — Reduce Large Scan File Sizes
description: Scanned PDFs are often 5–10x larger than they need to be. This tool compresses scanned document files while keeping text legible and signatures readable.
steps: Open JustUse.me Compress PDF
Upload your scanned PDF
Choose Medium or High Compression — scanned files benefit most from higher settings
Download the compressed file and verify readability

faq.q: Why are scanned PDFs so much larger than regular PDFs?
faq.a: Scanned PDFs store pages as images, not text. Each page is a high-resolution photo, making the file large even for simple text documents.

faq.q: Will the text in my scanned document stay readable after compression?
faq.a: Yes. Even at high compression, standard document text and signatures remain legible. Very fine print may soften slightly.

faq.q: Can I compress a scanned PDF that has multiple pages?
faq.a: Yes. There is no page count restriction. Upload your multi-page scanned document and compress the whole file at once.

title: PDF to JPG High Quality — Convert Without Losing Detail
description: Convert PDF pages to high-resolution JPG images suitable for printing, editing, or sharing. Output at 300 DPI to preserve every detail from the original.
steps: Open JustUse.me PDF to JPG
Upload your PDF file
Select High Quality output resolution
Download your JPG images — one per page

faq.q: What resolution are the exported JPG images?
faq.a: High quality exports use 300 DPI, which is suitable for print and detailed viewing.

faq.q: Will the text in my PDF be sharp in the JPG output?
faq.a: Yes. At high quality settings, text renders clearly and vector graphics stay crisp.

faq.q: Can I convert a PDF with multiple pages to separate JPG files?
faq.a: Yes. Each page is saved as its own JPG file, and you can download all of them as a ZIP archive.

title: Convert All PDF Pages to JPG — Batch Export Every Page
description: Extract every page from a PDF as a separate JPG file in one go. Download all pages as a ZIP file — no page selection needed.
steps: Go to JustUse.me PDF to JPG
Upload your multi-page PDF
The tool automatically converts every page
Download the ZIP file containing all JPG images

faq.q: Is there a page limit for batch PDF to JPG conversion?
faq.a: You can convert PDFs with up to 50 pages in a single upload.

faq.q: Are all pages exported as separate JPG files?
faq.a: Yes. Each PDF page becomes its own numbered JPG file inside the downloaded ZIP.

faq.q: Can I select only specific pages instead of converting all?
faq.a: The tool converts all pages by default. To convert specific pages, split the PDF first to isolate the pages you need.

title: PDF to Image for Presentation — Import Slides into PowerPoint or Keynote
description: Convert PDF slides to JPG images so you can import them into PowerPoint, Keynote, or Google Slides. Each slide becomes a clean, editable image.
steps: Open JustUse.me PDF to JPG
Upload your PDF presentation
Convert — each slide becomes a separate JPG
Import the JPG files into PowerPoint, Keynote, or Google Slides as image slides

faq.q: Can I insert a PDF slide directly into PowerPoint?
faq.a: PowerPoint does not natively import PDFs as editable slides. Converting to JPG first lets you insert each slide as an image.

faq.q: Will the slide layout and fonts look correct after conversion?
faq.a: Yes. The JPG captures the PDF exactly as it appears — fonts, images, and layout are all preserved as a flat image.

faq.q: What resolution should I use for presentation slides?
faq.a: Use high quality (300 DPI) for screen presentations and printing. Standard quality (150 DPI) is fine for screen-only viewing.

title: PDF to JPG Without Installing Software — Convert Online, Free
description: Convert PDF pages to JPG directly in your browser — no Adobe Acrobat, no desktop app, no installation. Works on Windows, Mac, and mobile.
steps: Open JustUse.me PDF to JPG in any browser
Upload your PDF from your computer or phone
Click Convert — the tool processes it in your browser
Download your JPG files immediately

faq.q: Do I need to install any software or browser extension?
faq.a: No. JustUse.me runs entirely in your browser. Nothing to install, update, or uninstall.

faq.q: Does this work on Windows without Adobe Acrobat?
faq.a: Yes. The tool works in Chrome, Edge, Firefox, or Safari on any Windows version — Adobe Acrobat not required.

faq.q: Can I convert a PDF to JPG on a Chromebook?
faq.a: Yes. Chromebooks support modern browsers, so JustUse.me works without any additional software.

title: Batch Convert PDF to JPG — Multiple PDFs at Once
description: Convert multiple PDF files to JPG images in a single session without uploading one at a time. Saves time when processing large document sets.
steps: Open JustUse.me PDF to JPG
Upload multiple PDF files at once using the file picker
The tool converts each PDF and extracts all pages as JPG images
Download all converted images as a single ZIP archive

faq.q: How many PDFs can I convert in one batch?
faq.a: You can upload multiple PDFs in a single session. Each PDF is processed and all pages are exported as JPGs.

faq.q: Will I get one ZIP for all files or a separate ZIP per PDF?
faq.a: All converted images are packaged into one ZIP file for easy download.

faq.q: Is batch PDF to JPG conversion free?
faq.a: Yes. Batch conversion is included at no cost — no account or payment needed.

title: Compress Image for Website — Faster Load Times, Smaller Files
description: Reduce image file sizes before uploading to your website or CMS. Smaller images mean faster page loads and better Core Web Vitals scores.
steps: Open JustUse.me Compress Image
Upload your JPG or PNG files
Adjust the quality slider to balance size and sharpness
Download compressed images and upload to your website

faq.q: What image format should I use for websites — JPG or PNG?
faq.a: Use JPG for photos and PNG for graphics with transparent backgrounds. JPG files compress much smaller for photographic content.

faq.q: How small should website images be?
faq.a: Aim for under 200KB per image for standard web use. Hero images can go up to 400KB if needed for quality.

faq.q: Will compressing images affect my Google rankings?
faq.a: Yes, positively. Faster page load times improve Core Web Vitals, which Google uses as a ranking factor. Smaller images directly help.

title: Compress Image Without Losing Quality — Lossless Optimization
description: Reduce image file size while keeping the image looking identical to the original. Uses smart compression to strip metadata and optimize encoding without degrading pixels.
steps: Go to JustUse.me Compress Image
Upload your JPG or PNG image
Set quality to 85–90% for near-lossless results
Download and compare — the images should look identical at normal viewing sizes

faq.q: What quality percentage gives the best balance of size and sharpness?
faq.a: 85% quality typically reduces file size by 40–60% with no visible quality difference to the human eye.

faq.q: Is PNG compression always lossless?
faq.a: Yes. PNG compression is lossless by design — file size reduces but image data is preserved exactly.

faq.q: Will color accuracy be affected by compression?
faq.a: At 80% quality and above, color accuracy is preserved for standard viewing. Only very aggressive compression (below 60%) causes visible color shifts.

title: Compress Image for Email Attachment — Shrink Photos Before Sending
description: Reduce photo file sizes so they send quickly and do not hit email attachment limits. Keeps images looking sharp for the recipient.
steps: Open JustUse.me Compress Image
Upload the photos you want to email
Choose a quality level — 80% is good for email photos
Download the smaller images and attach them to your email

faq.q: How small should images be for email?
faq.a: Keep each image under 1MB for email. Most recipients will view photos on screen, where high-resolution files add no benefit.

faq.q: Can I compress multiple images at once for an email?
faq.a: Yes. Upload multiple images in one session and download them all as a ZIP file.

faq.q: Will the recipient notice the image is compressed?
faq.a: At 80% quality, the difference is imperceptible on screen. Only side-by-side pixel-level comparison would reveal it.

title: Bulk Compress Images Online — Process Multiple Files at Once
description: Compress dozens of images in a single upload instead of processing them one by one. Useful for product photo batches, blog images, or any large collection.
steps: Open JustUse.me Compress Image
Select multiple images using the file picker or drag them all in at once
Set your quality preference — it applies to all images
Download all compressed images as a ZIP file

faq.q: How many images can I compress in one batch?
faq.a: You can upload and compress multiple images in a single session without reloading the page.

faq.q: Can I set different quality levels for different images in a batch?
faq.a: The quality setting applies uniformly to the whole batch. For individual control, process images separately.

faq.q: What formats are supported for bulk compression?
faq.a: JPG and PNG are fully supported. Upload mixed batches and each file is handled appropriately.

title: Compress Image Under 100KB — Hit Strict Size Requirements
description: Reduce image files to under 100KB for job applications, forms, and portals with strict size limits. Control the output size with the quality slider.
steps: Open JustUse.me Compress Image
Upload your image
Lower the quality slider until the estimated file size falls below 100KB
Download and verify the file size before uploading to the portal

faq.q: Why do applications require images under 100KB?
faq.a: Government portals, job boards, and visa applications enforce size limits to reduce storage costs and processing time.

faq.q: Will my photo still look acceptable on a form or application?
faq.a: For profile photos and ID-style images, 100KB is more than enough for clear results at standard display sizes.

faq.q: What if my photo is too large even at maximum compression?
faq.a: Resize the image to smaller pixel dimensions first, then compress. A passport-size photo at 400x500px compresses well under 100KB.

title: Convert iPhone HEIC Photos to JPG — Free, No App Needed
description: Turn iPhone HEIC photos into universally compatible JPG files. Works in your browser — no app download, no iCloud account required.
steps: Open JustUse.me HEIC to JPG on your computer or phone
Upload your HEIC photos from your iPhone, iCloud, or computer
The tool converts them to JPG automatically
Download the JPG files — ready to share or upload anywhere

faq.q: Why does my iPhone save photos as HEIC instead of JPG?
faq.a: HEIC is Apple's default format because it stores the same quality as JPG at half the file size, saving space on your device.

faq.q: Can I share HEIC photos with someone who has an Android phone?
faq.a: Most Android phones cannot open HEIC files. Convert to JPG first for universal compatibility.

faq.q: Will the converted JPG look the same as the original HEIC photo?
faq.a: Yes. The conversion preserves image quality, colors, and details from the original HEIC file.

title: Batch Convert HEIC to JPG — Convert Multiple Photos at Once
description: Convert an entire folder of HEIC photos to JPG in one go instead of converting one at a time. Download all converted files as a single ZIP.
steps: Open JustUse.me HEIC to JPG
Select all your HEIC files at once using the file picker
The tool converts every file simultaneously
Download all JPG files in a single ZIP archive

faq.q: How many HEIC files can I convert in one batch?
faq.a: You can upload and convert multiple HEIC files at once. Large batches may take a moment to process.

faq.q: Are all converted JPGs included in a single download?
faq.a: Yes. All converted JPG files are packaged into one ZIP file for easy download.

faq.q: Do the JPG filenames match the original HEIC filenames?
faq.a: Yes. Converted files keep the same name as the source file, just with a .jpg extension.

title: HEIC to JPG Without an App — Convert Directly in Your Browser
description: Convert HEIC files to JPG without downloading any software or app. Works on Mac, Windows, and iPhone through a standard web browser.
steps: Open JustUse.me HEIC to JPG in Chrome, Safari, or Firefox
Upload your HEIC files from your computer or phone
Conversion happens automatically in the browser
Download the JPG files — no app installation was needed at any step

faq.q: Can I convert HEIC to JPG on Windows without any software?
faq.a: Yes. Windows does not natively support HEIC, but JustUse.me handles it in your browser without any installation.

faq.q: Do I need to install a codec or extension to view HEIC files on Windows?
faq.a: Not for this tool. You can upload HEIC files directly even if your PC cannot preview them natively.

faq.q: Is there a Mac app that converts HEIC to JPG?
faq.a: Mac Preview can export HEIC to JPG, but for batch conversion without software, JustUse.me handles multiple files faster.

title: HEIC to JPG Without Losing Quality — Full-Resolution Conversion
description: Convert HEIC photos to JPG at maximum quality so you keep every detail from the original iPhone photo. No compression applied unless you choose it.
steps: Open JustUse.me HEIC to JPG
Upload your HEIC photo
Select maximum quality output (100%)
Download the full-resolution JPG

faq.q: Does converting HEIC to JPG always reduce quality?
faq.a: At 100% JPG quality, the visible difference from the original HEIC is negligible. Some metadata like depth maps may not transfer.

faq.q: Is HEIC actually better quality than JPG?
faq.a: HEIC and JPG can store the same visual quality, but HEIC uses more efficient compression. At equivalent quality settings, they look identical.

faq.q: Will the megapixel count change after conversion?
faq.a: No. The converted JPG has the same pixel dimensions as the original HEIC file.

title: Open HEIC on Windows — Convert to JPG for Full Compatibility
description: Windows cannot open HEIC files by default. Convert your HEIC photos to JPG in your browser and open them in any Windows app — no codec purchase needed.
steps: Open JustUse.me HEIC to JPG in your Windows browser
Upload the HEIC files you received or transferred from an iPhone
Download the converted JPG files
Open the JPGs in Windows Photos, Paint, or any other app

faq.q: Why can't Windows open HEIC files?
faq.a: HEIC uses Apple's format. Windows requires a paid codec from the Microsoft Store to open HEIC natively. Converting to JPG is a free alternative.

faq.q: My colleague sent me HEIC photos by email — how do I open them on Windows?
faq.a: Save the attachments, then upload them to JustUse.me HEIC to JPG. Download the converted JPGs and open them normally.

faq.q: Will the converted JPG open in older Windows versions like Windows 10?
faq.a: Yes. JPG is universally supported on all Windows versions going back decades.

title: Resize Image for Instagram — Get the Right Dimensions
description: Resize photos to Instagram's recommended dimensions for feed posts, stories, and reels. Avoid cropping surprises by preparing the exact pixel size before uploading.
steps: Open JustUse.me Resize Image
Upload your photo
Enter the Instagram dimensions: 1080x1080 (square), 1080x1350 (portrait), or 1080x608 (landscape)
Download and upload to Instagram

faq.q: What is the best image size for an Instagram feed post?
faq.a: 1080x1080px for square, 1080x1350px for portrait, and 1080x608px for landscape. Instagram may compress larger images.

faq.q: What size should Instagram Stories be?
faq.a: Instagram Stories should be 1080x1920px (9:16 aspect ratio) for full-screen display.

faq.q: Will resizing stretch or distort my photo?
faq.a: The tool lets you maintain aspect ratio during resizing. If the target size has a different ratio, you can choose to crop or add padding.

title: Resize Photo for Passport — Meet Official ID Photo Requirements
description: Resize your photo to meet passport and visa application requirements. Supports standard sizes including 2x2 inch (US), 35x45mm (UK/EU), and others.
steps: Open JustUse.me Resize Image
Upload a clear, front-facing photo
Enter the required dimensions for your country's passport (e.g., 600x600px for 2x2 inches at 300 DPI)
Download and use for your application

faq.q: What size is a US passport photo?
faq.a: US passports require a 2x2 inch photo (51x51mm), which is 600x600 pixels at 300 DPI.

faq.q: What size is a UK passport photo?
faq.a: UK passport photos must be 35x45mm, which is approximately 413x531 pixels at 300 DPI.

faq.q: Can I print the resized photo myself?
faq.a: Yes. Download the resized image and print it at a pharmacy or at home on photo paper. Ensure your printer is set to 300 DPI.

title: Resize Image to Exact Dimensions — Pixel-Perfect Custom Size
description: Enter any custom width and height in pixels and the tool resizes your image precisely. Useful for design specs, upload requirements, and print templates.
steps: Open JustUse.me Resize Image
Upload your image
Enter the exact width and height in pixels you need
Download the resized image at your specified dimensions

faq.q: Can I specify exact pixel dimensions for the output?
faq.a: Yes. Enter any width and height in pixels. The tool outputs the image at exactly those dimensions.

faq.q: What happens if my target dimensions have a different aspect ratio?
faq.a: You can choose to stretch to fit, crop to fill, or add padding to preserve the original aspect ratio.

faq.q: Can I resize an image to inches or centimeters instead of pixels?
faq.a: Enter pixels and use the DPI to calculate: multiply inches by DPI (e.g., 4 inches at 300 DPI = 1200px).

title: Resize Image for LinkedIn — Profile Photo and Banner Dimensions
description: Resize profile photos and cover banners to LinkedIn's recommended dimensions for a professional appearance. Avoids blurry or cropped profile pictures.
steps: Open JustUse.me Resize Image
Upload your photo or banner image
Enter the correct LinkedIn dimensions: 400x400px for profile photo, 1584x396px for banner
Download and upload to your LinkedIn profile

faq.q: What is the ideal LinkedIn profile photo size?
faq.a: LinkedIn recommends 400x400px. The photo displays as a circle, so keep your face centered and avoid tight crops.

faq.q: What size should my LinkedIn background banner be?
faq.a: LinkedIn banner dimensions are 1584x396px. Use a wide landscape image for best results.

faq.q: Why does my LinkedIn profile photo look blurry after uploading?
faq.a: Uploading an image smaller than 400x400px causes blurring when LinkedIn scales it up. Always start with at least 400x400px.

title: Bulk Resize Images Online — Resize Multiple Photos at Once
description: Resize an entire batch of images to the same dimensions without processing them one by one. Saves time for product photos, thumbnails, and content batches.
steps: Open JustUse.me Resize Image
Upload multiple images using the file picker or drag them in
Enter the target width and height to apply to all images
Download all resized images as a ZIP file

faq.q: Can I resize multiple images to the same size in one batch?
faq.a: Yes. Set your target dimensions once and the tool applies them to every uploaded image.

faq.q: What if my images have different aspect ratios?
faq.a: You can choose how to handle mismatched ratios — stretch, crop, or pad each image to fit the target dimensions.

faq.q: How many images can I resize in one session?
faq.a: You can upload and resize multiple images at once. All resized files download together in a ZIP.
